key_insight: Explicitly sampling non-ideal configurational entropy, together with vibrational entropy, yields more accurate binary alloy phase diagrams than ideal-mixing assumptions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical atomistic workflow for predicting order-disorder transitions and phase stability in alloy design, helping narrow uncertainty in composition-temperature maps.
method: Alchemical thermodynamic integration with composition-dependent transformations
Monte Carlo identity-exchange moves plus molecular dynamics to capture configurational and vibrational entropy
ACE potentials trained on DFT (LDA, PBE, r2SCAN) to compute free energies and phase diagrams
evidence_signal: For AuCu, explicit configurational sampling lowers the predicted order-disorder transition from about 810 K to 710 K, closer to the 683 K experimental value, and widens the solid-solution stability range.
watch_item: Agreement is strongly dependent on the exchange-correlation functional, so improved entropy sampling does not by itself guarantee predictive accuracy.
paper_title: Computing binary alloy phase diagrams with explicit configurational and vibrational entropy
